title: Phlegmariurus oceanianus
intro: Phlegmariurus oceanianus, also known as Oceanian Horsetail or Australian Phlegmariurus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lycopodiaceae, characterized by its slender, upright stems and scale-like leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Bioactive constituents of Phlegmariurus oceanianus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat bronchitis, coughs, and other respiratory problems.
